passage:
A study examined average daily screen time (in hours) among teenagers in four countries and compared it to the percentage of teenagers in each country reporting high levels of loneliness.

| Country | Avg. Daily Screen Time (hrs) | % Reporting High Loneliness |
|---|---|---|
| Country A | 2.1 | 14 |
| Country B | 4.3 | 22 |
| Country C | 6.8 | 31 |
| Country D | 3.5 | 19 |

A researcher claimed that higher screen time is associated with higher rates of reported loneliness among teenagers.
question: Which statement about the data most directly supports the researcher's claim?

choices.A: Country A has the lowest screen time and the lowest loneliness percentage, while Country C has the highest screen time and the highest loneliness percentage.
choices.B: Country D has lower screen time than Country B and a lower loneliness rate than Country C.
choices.C: Countries B and D have similar loneliness rates despite having different screen times.
choices.D: Country C has the highest screen time of the four countries.

explanation: The researcher claims higher screen time is associated with higher loneliness. Choice A directly supports this by identifying the two extremes in the dataset: Country A (2.1 hrs screen time, 14% loneliness) and Country C (6.8 hrs, 31% loneliness). The pattern holds at both ends—lowest screen time paired with lowest loneliness, highest screen time with highest loneliness—providing the most compelling evidence for the association. Choice B is a partial comparison that does not directly compare the most relevant variables across the full spectrum. Choice C notes a case where the relationship is imperfect, which could actually weaken the claim. Choice D notes only a single variable for Country C without linking it to loneliness.
